title: Stop First. Every Second After That Costs More.
body: When a Ground Engaging Tool separates from a bucket or dozer blade, the clock starts immediately — muck moves fast through the load-and-haul cycle. The ADW procedure runs four phases in sequence: Stop, Report, Recover, Verify. Skipping or rushing any phase risks a tooth reaching the primary crusher, which means liner damage, plant downtime, and a safety event that will take days to explain.
keyPoints: The operator must stop the equipment the moment GET loss is suspected — not after the pass, not after the load. Stop immediately and notify the supervisor with time and GPS location.

The Missing GET Reporting sheet must capture Equipment ID, operator name, time, GPS coordinates, and the type and count of missing GET. Incomplete reporting means Survey can't pinpoint the search zone.

If the piece isn't recovered after exhaustive effort, senior management must inform the client representative before it reaches the crusher — and the ROM Pad loader operator becomes the last human interception point, examining every feed load.
Pre-start GET inspections on all mobile equipment and daily service checks are the first line of defence. Operators trained to feel and hear a lost tooth prevent the event before it starts.
apply: Before first pass on your primary loading units today, physically verify that the GET inspection was signed off on the pre-start sheets. Confirm the excavator operator knows the current procedure for suspected loss — ask them what they would do first. If anyone hesitates, that's your debrief topic at the next toolbox.
